summary: All 8 print-page screenshots render the print overlay with page labels and connector/card content where that tile intersects the W2 flowchart. This is visual proof that the print overlay is nonblank, but it is not a fidelity pass.

observations.observation: Mostly blank tile with one horizontal connector and page label A1.
Contains a visible card at the right edge plus connector; card is clipped by the tile boundary.
Contains a partial card at the left edge plus connector; card is clipped by the tile boundary.
Mostly blank tile with one horizontal connector and page label A4.
Largest screenshot; multiple cards and connectors are visible, with bottom-row cards clipped by the tile boundary.
Contains lower-edge partial cards and connectors; content is clipped by the tile boundary.
Contains right-edge and bottom-edge partial cards plus connectors; content is clipped by the tile boundary.
Contains left-edge and bottom-edge partial cards plus connectors; content is clipped by the tile boundary.

phase0Conclusion: The screenshots are sufficient as print visual proof for Phase 0 evidence capture, but they do not close print fidelity. A future visual-diff or print-layout pass should decide whether edge clipping is acceptable tiling behavior or needs a layout adjustment.
